what_it_does: Documents a critical defect in the decision surface: 10 of 24 decision rows lacked a subtype, so they had no resolved owner and were silently omitted from Tim's operator queue; it records the diagnosis, fix, and remaining residuals.
observations: Severity was CRITICAL because real pending decisions were invisible on the live surface.
Status is CLOSED after all 24 rows were tagged and subtype was made required in the schema.
The decision schema originally required only id, meaning, and options; owner, card_variant, and required_elements all resolve solely via subtype.
The initial pass misclassified 7 rows as visual/DNA-owned; reading them showed only 4 were design-language and 3 were held-substrate decisions owned by Tim.
The worst instance was substrate-home, a frame-first keystone card that was invisible.
Root-cause fixes: tag all 10, make subtype required, update AGENTS.md authoring contract.

Residuals include 4 design-language rows that are decided but lack decision_take marks, a theorem-fork grounding_source gap on cube-3d and dimension-meaning, and a marks tool failure blocking verification.
standouts: Silent drop of decisions from Tim's queue is the exact failure mode the surface exists to prevent.
The worst invisible row was substrate-home, a frame-first keystone.
The author explicitly corrects an earlier misclassification, emphasizing 'classify by reading the row, never by the filename'.
The same silent-gap class reappears as a theorem-fork grounding_source residual.
conventions: Follows an incident-report structure with Found, Severity, Status, Defect, Fix, and Residuals sections.
Uses decision-surface vocabulary: owner, card_variant, required_elements, decision_subtypes registry.
Applies schema-gate discipline: tag all rows before making subtype required to avoid a C5 break.
Treats render refinements (design-language card variants) as follow-ups separate from owner resolution.
concerns: 4 design-language rows are already decided but lack decision_take marks, so the new subtype could resurface settled calls as pending.
The marks tool fails loud on an unrelated role-schema error, blocking self-verification of those decided marks.
theorem-fork grounding_source resolves to nothing on cube-3d and dimension-meaning, repeating the silent-gap pattern.
rerank-loadout embeds a load-authorization inside an option, hinting a future capability/authorize-hybrid subtype may be needed.
notes: A closed critical incident report about 10 decision rows that were invisible because they lacked a subtype, plus the schema fix and remaining follow-ups.
questions: What exactly do C5-class break and C2.1 field set refer to in this codebase?
What is the theorem-fork ai_uncertainty_caveat and how does it differ from a general AI-inference caveat?
Who or what are Tim and DNA in this context, and what is the live operator surface?
What is the decision_subtypes registry format and where is it defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a subtype-coverage gap and its closure; the file does exactly that, including diagnosis, fix, and residuals.
apparent_themes: Read the content, not the filename
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the decision surface complete and fail-loud by enforcing subtype, documenting the ownership rule, and surfacing any remaining work so the same silent drop cannot recur.
novelty: It combines incident postmortem, schema governance, render semantics, and an explicit self-correction about classification methodology in one decision-record.
contribution: A concrete closed-defect record that preserves the root-cause fix (subtype required), the authoring contract, the corrected owner classification for all 24 rows, and a set of residuals that a fused system can use to verify queue completeness and grounding-source coverage.
summary_for_embedding: A critical incident report documenting that 10 of 24 decision rows lacked a subtype and were silently excluded from Tim's queue, the closure by making subtype required, and residuals around decided marks and theorem-fork grounding sources.
intention_for_embedding: To record why subtype became required, how the missing rows were recovered, and what follow-ups remain before the next release bounce.
